content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Amendment fr-10-1e1f4d3a correctly identifies that reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage never assigns MilestoneStatus::Failed — the else-if chain at lines 906-925 can produce Running, Paused, Completed, or Ready, but never Failed. So unrecoverable run failures routed through record_bead_completion get silently downgraded to Paused. Amendment fr-10-7f55af74 misdiagnoses its failure mechanism: when a snapshot enters reconciliation as Paused, line 907-908 short-circuits and preserves Paused without re-evaluating whether all beads are complete, so the Completed status is never produced and the repair path never reaches the transition bridge function at all. Adding a Paused->Completed bridge would not fix the issue.

## Votes

- `fr-10-1e1f4d3a`: Accept
  Verified in reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage (lines 896-926): the else-if chain handles active beads (→Running), already-Paused (→preserve Paused), all-beads-complete-no-failures (→Completed), running-no-active-bead (→Paused), and has-runs-not-terminal (→Paused/Ready). MilestoneStatus::Failed is never assigned by any branch. When a bead finishes as TaskRunOutcome::Failed, active_beads becomes empty, failed_beads > 0 blocks the Completed check (line 912), and line 916-918 converts Running to Paused. The milestone-level Failed state is therefore unreachable on the normal sync path despite existing in the enum and being referenced in transition reasoning strings. This is a genuine lifecycle defect: distinguishing retryable bead failure from unrecoverable milestone failure and committing the Failed transition atomically is a correct and necessary fix.
- `fr-10-7f55af74`: Reject
  The amendment claims that repairing lineage can recompute the snapshot as Completed via lines 909-915, but this is blocked by the else-if chain structure: line 907-908 checks `snapshot.status == MilestoneStatus::Paused` before the completion check at lines 909-915. When a snapshot enters reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage as Paused, it short-circuits and preserves Paused without evaluating whether progress is 100%. The repair path therefore never produces a Paused→Completed transition for build_reconciled_transition_events to reject — the stale Paused status is retained earlier in the pipeline. A fix limited to synthesizing a Paused→Completed bridge in transition synthesis would be incomplete and misleading; the actual bug is in snapshot reconciliation preserving Paused unconditionally. The planner position's diagnosis is more accurate here.
